name: Phyllis Diller
blurb: Phyllis Diller was an American stand-up comedian who became one of the first women to headline as a solo comic, building an act on rapid-fire self-deprecating one-liners, a wild fright-wig persona, and an explosive.
bio: Phyllis Diller was an American stand-up comedian who became one of the first women to headline as a solo comic, building an act on rapid-fire self-deprecating one-liners, a wild fright-wig persona, and an explosive cackle. A favorite on The Ed Sullivan Show and a frequent Bob Hope collaborator, she opened doors for generations of female comedians.

timeline.ev: Makes her professional stand-up debut at the Purple Onion in San Francisco
First national TV appearance on Groucho Marxs You Bet Your Life
Debuts on The Ed Sullivan Show with her Thanksgiving turkey routine
Co-stars with Bob Hope in Boy, Did I Get a Wrong Number!
Becomes a semi-regular on The Hollywood Squares
Gives her final stand-up performance in Las Vegas

accolades: One of the first solo female comedians to become a household name
Cited as an influence by Joan Rivers, Ellen DeGeneres and Roseanne Barr
Her gag file is preserved in the Smithsonian Institution
